name: Penha Longa-Sintra Cycling Ruta — Sintra-Cascais Natural Park
name_original: Circuito ciclável Penha Longa-Sintra — Parque Natural de Sintra-Cascais
region_intro: Touring cycling around Sintra (São Pedro De Penaferrim) offers a diverse landscape within the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park. The region features the Sintra Mountains, providing varied terrain from challenging paths to paved routes, and a distinct microclimate. Cyclists can explore routes winding through ancient forests, along rugged coastlines, and past historic sites. This area is characterized by significant elevation changes and proximity to the Atlantic Ocean. Best touring cycling routes around Sintra (São Pedro De Penaferrim) The most popular touring cycling route is Penha Longa-Sintra Cycling Ruta — Sintra-Cascais Natural Park, a 21.0 miles (33.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 9 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers significant elevation gain within the natural park. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Praia de São Julião – Praia do Sul (Ericeira) loop from Sintra, a difficult 31.8 miles (51.2 km) path. This route features coastal views and challenging climbs. Local touring cyclists also love the Wood Path with Small Bridges – Tapada de Monserrate loop from Quinta da Beloura I, a 14.1 miles (22.8 km) trail leading through forested areas, often completed in about 2 hours. Touring cycling around Sintra (São Pedro De Penaferrim) is defined by the Sintra Mountains, dense woodlands, and impressive coastal paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging routes with significant elevation. The routes in Sintra (São Pedro De Penaferrim) are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 4000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Sintra (São Pedro De Penaferrim)'s varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Sintra (São Pedro De Penaferrim)?
A: There are over 150 touring cycling routes available around Sintra (São Pedro De Penaferrim). These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 300 reviews.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Sintra (São Pedro De Penaferrim)?
A: The Sintra region benefits from a distinct microclimate, but generally, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant conditions for touring cycling. The evergreen forests provide shade, which can be welcome during warmer months, but some sections can be steep and narrow, requiring careful navigation regardless of the season.

Q: What are the typical terrain and elevation challenges for touring cyclists in this region?
A: Touring cycling in Sintra (São Pedro De Penaferrim) is characterized by significant elevation changes within the Sintra Mountains. You'll encounter a variety of terrain, from challenging mountain paths and wide dirt roads to paved routes. Coastal paths, like the Cascais Coastal Cycle Path, tend to be flatter, but many routes involve substantial climbs. For example, routes can feature elevation gains of over 700 meters, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2283625210/penha-longa-sintra-cycling-ruta-sintra-cascais-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penha Longa-Sintra Cycling Ruta — Sintra-Cascais Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or less experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, while many routes in Sintra (São Pedro De Penaferrim) are challenging, there are 27 easy and 63 moderate touring cycling routes available. These routes offer less demanding terrain and elevation, making them suitable for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. The Cascais Coastal Cycle Path, though not directly in São Pedro De Penaferrim, is a good example of a predominantly flat and segregated path in the wider region.

Q: Where can I find challenging touring cycling routes in Sintra (São Pedro De Penaferrim)?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 68 difficult routes in the area. These often feature significant elevation gains and varied terrain. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17298782" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Praia de São Julião – Praia do Sul (Ericeira) loop from Sintra&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 50 km with more than 800 meters of climbing, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32395080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;National Palace of Sintra – View of the Sintra coastline loop from Portela de Sintra&lt;/a&gt;, offering over 700 meters of ascent.

Q: What natural features and viewpoints can I expect to see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty, offering stunning panoramic vistas. You can expect to see the lush Sintra Mountains, rugged coastlines, and dense woodlands. Notable viewpoints include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/131009" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabo da Roca — Westernmost point of mainland Europe&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/131007" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peninha Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt;, which provides breathtaking 360-degree views of the mountains and Atlantic Ocean. You might also encounter natural features like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/463773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ursa Beach&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/345009" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mula River Dam&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there historical landmarks or palaces accessible by touring bike in Sintra (São Pedro De Penaferrim)?
A: Yes, many of Sintra's iconic attractions are integrated into or accessible via cycling routes. You can cycle past or near sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/205133" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Moors&lt;/a&gt;, and the Sintra National Palace. While direct access to some palaces like Pena Palace can be challenging due to steep gradients, routes often offer scenic ascents leading to their vicinity. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/337190" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monserrate Palace and Gardens&lt;/a&gt; are also part of longer cycling routes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Sintra (São Pedro De Penaferrim) are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32220388" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wood Path with Small Bridges – Tapada de Monserrate loop from Quinta da Beloura I&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through forested areas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32284214" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Azenhas do Mar – View of Azenhas do Mar loop from Portela de Sintra&lt;/a&gt; is another popular circular option.

Q: Is it possible to find cafes or places to rest along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is welcoming to cyclists. São Pedro de Penaferrim itself is known for local cafes where you can stop for a break. The presence of 'refúgio do Ciclista' (cyclist's lounge) in the broader Sintra area further indicates that amenities for cyclists are available along various routes.

Q: What are the options for parking when starting a touring cycling route?
A: Parking options vary depending on your chosen starting point. In Sintra and surrounding towns, there are public parking areas, though they can be busy, especially during peak season. It's advisable to check specific parking availability for your chosen route's trailhead or starting village.

Q: Can I reach the touring cycling routes using public transport?
A: Sintra is well-connected by public transport, particularly by train from Lisbon, making it a convenient starting point for many routes. From the Sintra train station, you can often access various cycling paths or connect to local buses that serve different parts of the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park, allowing you to reach your desired trailhead.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Sintra (São Pedro De Penaferrim)?
A: While many routes feature significant elevation, there are 27 easy touring cycling routes that could be suitable for families, depending on the children's cycling experience. The Cascais Coastal Cycle Path, though outside the immediate São Pedro De Penaferrim area, is a well-known family-friendly option due to its flat, segregated nature. For routes within the Sintra Mountains, look for those explicitly marked as 'easy' and consider e-bikes for navigating steeper sections.

Q: Are touring cycling routes in Sintra (São Pedro De Penaferrim) suitable for cycling with dogs?
A: The suitability of routes for cycling with dogs depends on the specific trail and your dog's fitness. Many trails within the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park are unpaved and pass through natural areas, which can be enjoyable for dogs. However, always ensure your dog is on a leash, respect local regulations regarding pets in protected areas, and be mindful of traffic on shared roads. Consider shorter, less strenuous routes for your dog's comfort.
tips: The Peninha Sanctuary, located in the Sintra Mountains in the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park, in Portugal, is located at an altitude of 448 meters, offering views over the coast and inland. In addition to a baroque chapel completed in 1710, the site is home to the Peninha Palace, dating from 1918, and remains of a hermitage. Currently, the interiors of the chapel and palace are not open to visitors.
Wonderful view shortly after Azoia when driving towards Cascais.
